home.allDoneSub: Enjoy your day in the garden.
home.startFirstText: Add a garden photo, add plants and track caring for them.
home.demoHintSub: Tap the “+” in the bottom right to create your own. You can delete the demo any time in Settings.
monthTips: Plan planting for the next season
Plan the season in your kitchen

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.todayDoneSub: No overdue tasks or tasks due today 🌿
tasks.weekCalmSub: No tasks in the next 7 days.
tasks.noTasksSub: Add them in the detail of a spot in your garden.
tasks.noHistorySub: Once you complete your first task, it shows up here.

settings.title: Settings
settings.accountFoot: Your name appears in the greeting on the home screen. It is stored only on this device.
settings.appearanceFoot: Switch between the light and dark theme.
settings.languageFoot: Choose the app language. The choice is saved on this device.
settings.notifDenied: Notifications denied. Allow them in your browser settings.
settings.smtpWarning: ⚠️ SMTP has no credentials yet (GMAIL_FROM / GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD). The address can be saved, but emails won't be sent yet.
settings.notifFoot: Task summary. Push arrives even without the app open, email every Monday at 8:00.
settings.calendarFoot: Live subscription of seasonal tasks, planting, pruning and harvest across all gardens (refresh 1×/day).
settings.deleteDemoConfirm: Delete the demo garden? Demo tasks will be removed, your own data stays.
settings.dangerFoot: Reset only clears local settings. Deleting data is irreversible — back up first via Export.
settings.footClaim: Garden management in your pocket · version 1.0
settings.resetConfirm: Reset the app? It will clear local settings (theme, name, onboarding) on this device. Your gardens, plants and tasks stay untouched.
settings.deleteConfirm: Really delete ALL data? All gardens, plants, tasks, photos and care history will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.
settings.deletePrompt: To confirm, type in capitals: SMAZAT
settings.deletedGardens: Deleted {{count}} gardens and everything with them
settings.aboutFoot: Questions, ideas or bug reports — send them to the contact email. We reply within 3 working days.

gardens.title: Gardens
gardens.emptyDesc: Add an aerial photo or start from a template.
gardens.shareText: Check out my garden “{{name}}” in GardenPin
gardens.deleteConfirmPre: Do you really want to delete the garden
gardens.deleteWarning: The map, all pins ({{count}}) and their tasks will be deleted. This action cannot be undone.
gardens.renameHint: Edit growing conditions and the map in the garden detail.

gardenDetail.upscaleConfirm: Upscale 4× – processes the image server-side (bicubic). Continue?
gardenDetail.pinInsideGarden: Place the pin inside the garden
gardenDetail.deleteGardenConfirm: Really delete this garden with all pins and tasks?
gardenDetail.uploadAerialPrompt: Upload an aerial photo of your garden
gardenDetail.satelliteTip: 💡 No aerial photo of your own? Open the address in Google Maps satellite view, take a screenshot and upload it.
gardenDetail.screenshotHint: Take a screenshot and upload it as the map.
gardenDetail.hintBedMode: Tap and drag to create a bed
gardenDetail.hintMapMode: Tap the map to add · drag a pin to move
gardenDetail.polygonHint: ✂️ Drag the points to the corners of the garden. Click the white point in the middle = add. Double-click = delete.
gardenDetail.polygonHintTap: Tap a point to select it, then use the buttons.
gardenDetail.polygonHintSelected: Point selected — use the buttons below or drag to move.
gardenDetail.polygonAddTitle: Add point after the selected one (between neighbors)
gardenDetail.polygonConfirmTitle: Confirm and crop the photo to the garden shape
gardenDetail.polygonSelectFirst: Tap the point you want to delete first.
gardenDetail.polygonMinPoints: Polygon must have at least 3 points.
gardenDetail.bedToolTitle: Create a bed (click and drag across the map)
gardenDetail.polygonOutlineTitle: Outline the garden with a polygon
gardenDetail.revertCropTitle: Restore the original garden photo (before polygon crop)
gardenDetail.revertCropConfirm: Revert the photo to its state before cropping? The polygon outline will be cleared and you can redraw it.
gardenDetail.noPlaces: No places yet. Click the map above to add one.
gardenDetail.shareText: Check out my garden in GardenPin
gardenDetail.revokeConfirm: Revoke sharing? The link will stop working.
gardenDetail.shareDescription: Anyone with this link can view your garden, plants and upcoming tasks. No editing allowed.
gardenDetail.calendarConfigIntro: Choose what you want in your calendar. Then we'll generate a live link (webcal://).
gardenDetail.noPlantsInGarden: There are no plants in this garden yet.
gardenDetail.liveLinkInfo: Live link — the calendar updates itself (refresh 1 day). Changes to tasks are reflected automatically.
gardenDetail.googleCalendarUrlLabel: URL for Google Calendar (add via "From URL")
gardenDetail.growingConditionsHint: They affect the recommended task dates (a cold/warm location shifts the calendar).
gardenDetail.climateZoneDescribed: {{desc}} — recommended task dates will be adjusted for your location.

pin.confirmDeletePin: Really delete this place and all its tasks?
pin.tasksEmpty: No tasks. Add watering, pruning or transplanting via ＋.
pin.careEmpty: No care info. Assign a plant from the catalog or edit the notes.
pin.changePlantHint: Care history and tasks are preserved — only the plant species changes.
pin.toastShareNoGarden: This place isn't linked to any garden
pin.photosEmpty: No photos yet. Add the first plant photo.
pin.growthHintOnePhoto: Add another photo in a few weeks — you'll see how the plant has grown.
pin.harvestNotePlaceholder: e.g. first harvest of the season
pin.harvestEmpty: No harvest records yet. Record your first yield!
pin.forecastChartAria: Monthly harvest: last year vs this year
pin.forecastHintDecline: Behind last year so far. Try watering or fertilizing — the plant may need nutrients.
pin.issuesEmpty: No issues recorded yet. All clear 🌿
pin.issuesTreatmentPlaceholder: e.g. removed affected leaves, copper spray…

overview.title: Overview
overview.emptyText: You have no scheduled tasks in the next 14 days.

catalog.emptyText: Try changing the search term or filter.
catalog.noSeasonalTasks: No seasonal tasks in the database.
catalog.pickerHint: A pin will be created in the center of the garden map. You can move it later.

shared.unavailableText: The link has been revoked or does not exist.
shared.noPlants: There are no plants in this garden yet.
shared.noUpcoming: No scheduled tasks in the next 3 months.

onboarding.selectPlant: Pick a plant from the list
onboarding.welcomeText: Your garden journal. It keeps track of the main seasonal tasks all year round — pruning, repotting, fertilizing. We'll walk you through the first setup in a moment.
onboarding.zoneText: Based on your region we'll adjust the timing of seasonal tasks — spring arrives earlier in warm lowlands than in the mountains or the northeast.
onboarding.gardenText: Give it a name and upload an aerial photo — it'll help you find your way among the plants. The photo is optional.
onboarding.plantText: Pick your first plant from 321 species. GardenPin will suggest the main tasks and schedule them in the right months.
onboarding.doneScheduled_one: We've scheduled {{count}} seasonal task. GardenPin will remind you each month so you don't forget anything.
onboarding.doneScheduled_other: We've scheduled {{count}} seasonal tasks. GardenPin will remind you each month so you don't forget anything.
onboarding.doneScheduledForPlant_one: We've scheduled {{count}} seasonal task for {{plant}}. GardenPin will remind you each month so you don't forget anything.
onboarding.doneScheduledForPlant_other: We've scheduled {{count}} seasonal tasks for {{plant}}. GardenPin will remind you each month so you don't forget anything.
onboarding.doneNoTasks: Once you add plants, GardenPin will schedule the main seasonal tasks for you and remind you about them each month.
onboarding.demoTaskNote: Sample seasonal task. Tap the pin for details or ✓ to mark done.

recommended.zoneShiftLater: 📍 Adjusted for your location ({{zone}}) — dates shifted by +{{days}} days
recommended.zoneShiftEarlier: 📍 Adjusted for your location ({{zone}}) — dates moved earlier by {{days}} days
recommended.microColder: 🌡️ Cooler garden microclimate — dates shifted by +{{days}} days
recommended.microWarmer: 🌡️ Warmer garden microclimate — dates shifted by {{days}} days
recommended.addAll_other: + Add all {{count}} tasks for the year
recommended.pastThisYear: Already happened this year — will be scheduled for next year

seasonalCal.title: 📅 Seasonal calendar
seasonalCal.noneInYear: There are no tasks scheduled in {{year}}.
seasonalCal.tryOther: Try selecting another garden or year.

streak.startHint: Complete at least one task a day
streak.frozenTooltip: You can skip one day this week without losing your streak.
streak.frozenUsedTooltip: You've already used your freeze this week — a new one resets on Monday.

frost.tooltip: Scheduled on a frosty day ({{temp}} °C) — frost can damage tender seedlings

seasonWindow.tooltip: The seasonal window for this task has already passed — move it to next season, or still do it now.

weather.frostText_one: Cover your sensitive plants — you have {{count}}.
weather.frostText_other: Cover your sensitive plants — you have {{count}}.

seasonStats.title: 📊 Season {{year}}
seasonStats.emptyCurrent: No completed actions yet — get started!

yoy.title: Year-over-year comparison
yoy.missingTitle: ⏰ What you did last year but not yet this year

template.title: 🌱 Garden from template
template.intro: Choose a garden type and the template will prepare plant pins and their seasonal tasks. You can upload a map photo later.
template.summarySuffix: and all their recurring and seasonal tasks.

search.placeholder: Search for a plant, pin or garden…
search.promptSub: Start typing — I'll find plants, pins and gardens across the whole database.
search.noMatches: No matches for “{{query}}”. Try a different word.

members.title: Members
members.intro: Invite family or friends to help with the garden — they'll see the tasks and editors can pitch in.
members.emailHint: If you enter an email, we'll send the invite automatically. Otherwise copy the link manually.
members.shareText: Join me in the {{garden}} garden on GardenPin 🌿

invite.invalidText: This invitation doesn't exist or was revoked.
invite.note: Once accepted, this browser signs in as this member.

yearPlan.title: Year-round care plan
yearPlan.subtitle: {{plant}} — main seasonal tasks for the year ahead

bulkCare.title: Bulk care
bulkCare.subtitle: Schedule the same task for all plants of the same species at once
bulkCare.subtitleCategory: {{category}} — pick the species and create tasks at once
bulkCare.noCategories: No plant in this garden has a main seasonal task to schedule in bulk.

seasonMoment.title: Seasonal moment
seasonMoment.subtitle: Plan care for the whole garden by time of year
seasonMoment.subtitleMoment: {{moment}} — pick and create tasks at once
seasonMoment.noMoments: No seasonal moment has anything to schedule for this garden right now.
seasonMoment.springDesc: Pruning, fertilizing and planting — the garden wakes up.
seasonMoment.summerDesc: Shaping, pest checks and feeding in peak season.
seasonMoment.autumnDesc: Transplanting, mulching, frost protection and harvest.
seasonMoment.winterDesc: Winter pruning of fruit trees during the dormant season.
seasonMoment.allPlanned: You've already scheduled everything for this moment 🎉

phenology.tooltip: Timing tuned to this year's temperatures ({{temp}} °C vs. the long-term normal).

careHistory.tooltipLast: Last year you did this on a different day — shift the date to match.
careHistory.tooltipAvg_one: Your average over {{count}} year · last year {{last}}
careHistory.tooltipAvg_other: Your average over {{count}} years · last year {{last}}

careGap.title: Not planned yet this year
careGap.summary_one: {{count}} task from last year is still missing this year.
careGap.summary_other: {{count}} tasks from last year are still missing this year.
careGap.notes: Done {{date}} last year — not yet planned this year.

ageTasks.title: Pruning by plant age
ageTasks.guardMeta: Let the plant establish — hold off on pruning this year.
ageTasks.formativeMeta: Year {{age}} since planting — build the basic shape and framework.
ageTasks.rejuvenationMeta: Older woody plant — thin it out and rejuvenate it.

divisionTasks.title: Time to divide the clump
divisionTasks.metaAutumn: The clump is crowding and flowering less — dig it up and divide it in autumn.
divisionTasks.metaSpring: The clump is crowding and flowering less — dig it up and divide it in early spring.
divisionTasks.notes: Clump division (year {{age}} since planting) — restores flowering.

perennialCutback.title: Time to cut back
perennialCutback.subtitle: Cut back the dead top growth of the perennial or grass
perennialCutback.metaSpring: Leave it over winter (silhouette + crown protection) and cut it back in early spring before it sprouts.
perennialCutback.metaAutumn: After it dies back in autumn, cut the dead top growth down to the ground.
perennialCutback.notes: Seasonal cutback of the top growth — {{meta}}

hedgeTrim.title: Time to shape the hedge
hedgeTrim.subtitle: Trim the formal hedge so it stays dense and crisp
hedgeTrim.metaSummer1: First summer trim after the birds have nested — cut the new growth back to shape.
hedgeTrim.metaSummer2: Second trim in late summer — so the hedge goes into winter tidy.
hedgeTrim.notes: Summer shaping cut of the hedge — {{meta}}

sowingTasks.title: Sow from seed indoors
sowingTasks.subtitle: Start seedlings ahead of planting out
sowingTasks.notes: Plant out ~{{date}} — pre-grow from seed in a tray.

bulbPlanting.title: Time to plant the bulbs
bulbPlanting.subtitle: Plant in autumn so they bloom in spring
bulbPlanting.action: Plant the bulbs in the ground
bulbPlanting.notes: Autumn bulb planting (~{{depth}} cm deep) — they'll bloom in spring.

cuttingTasks.title: Time to take cuttings
cuttingTasks.subtitle: Propagate your plants for free from cuttings
cuttingTasks.instrSoftwood: From soft spring growth, ~10 cm, root in shade under cover.
cuttingTasks.instrSemiripe: From summer shoots woody at the base, with a heel, into a propagator.
cuttingTasks.instrHardwood: From ripe wood after leaf fall, ~20 cm, outdoors in open ground.

plantSupports.title: Time for supports
plantSupports.subtitle: Put supports in before the plant shoots up
plantSupports.instrRing: Set a grow-through (ring) support early in spring as the clump emerges — the shoots grow up through it and hide it.
plantSupports.instrStake: Push a stake in next to the tall stem and tie it in as it grows so it doesn't snap.
plantSupports.instrTrellis: Put up the trellis and tie in new spring growth early, before it hardens off.

graftingTasks.title: Time to graft
graftingTasks.subtitle: Graft your fruit trees onto a named variety
graftingTasks.instrSpring: Whip / rind / side graft — as sap starts to flow and the rootstock breaks bud.
graftingTasks.instrSummer: T-budding onto a dormant bud — while the bark lifts cleanly from the wood.

trunkWhitewash.title: Time to whitewash trunks
trunkWhitewash.subtitle: Protect the bark from winter sun and frost cracks
trunkWhitewash.action: Whitewash the trunk with lime paint
trunkWhitewash.instr: After leaf fall, before hard frosts — white reflects winter sun and prevents frost cracks (split bark).
trunkWhitewash.notes: Whitewash the trunk and scaffold branches with lime paint — protection against frost cracks.

fruitThinning.title: Time to thin the fruit
fruitThinning.subtitle: Thin the excess set so the remaining fruit grows larger
fruitThinning.instrPome: Leave 1 strongest fruit per cluster, 8–10 cm apart — the remaining fruit grows larger and branches don't break.
fruitThinning.instrStone: Thin the excess fruitlets to roughly 8 cm apart — a gentler thinning to keep the tree from biennial bearing.

peachLeafCurlSpray.title: Time to protect peach from leaf curl
peachLeafCurlSpray.subtitle: Preventive copper spray JUST BEFORE bud break — without it leaf curl destroys foliage and cuts yield by 50–80 %
peachLeafCurlSpray.instrStrong: Bordeaux mixture / copper fungicide — JUST BEFORE bud break (buds swelling, not yet open). Dry, calm day, T > 5 °C.
peachLeafCurlSpray.instrMild: Bordeaux mixture / copper fungicide — preventively before bud break. Dry, calm day, T > 5 °C.
peachLeafCurlSpray.notes: Bordeaux mixture / copper fungicide — JUST BEFORE bud break, on a dry day, T > 5 °C.

summerPruning.title: Time for summer pruning
summerPruning.subtitle: Sommerschnitt — light green pruning of this year's shoots tames growth, opens the canopy and helps fruit ripen
summerPruning.instr: Shorten this year's shoots by 1/3 above an outward-facing leaf, remove water sprouts and crowding shoots. Dry, calm day.
summerPruning.notes: Sommerschnitt — shorten shoots by 1/3, remove water sprouts and crowding growth.

summerRosePruning.title: Time for the summer rose prune
summerRosePruning.subtitle: Deadhead + light cutback after the first flush — triggers the second flowering wave in August–September
summerRosePruning.instr: Remove spent flower heads and cut the flowering shoot back by 1/3 above a healthy outward-facing 5-leaflet leaf. Remove blind (non-flowering) shoots.
summerRosePruning.notes: Deadhead spent blooms, cut shoots back 1/3 above an outward-facing 5-leaflet leaf — for the second flowering wave.

herbHarvest.title: Time to harvest the herb for drying
herbHarvest.subtitle: Aroma peaks just before flowering — pick, dry in the shade, store for winter
herbHarvest.action: Pick and dry leaves/tops for winter
herbHarvest.instr: Harvest on a dry day around noon, just before full bloom or at flowering onset — essential oils and aroma are at their peak. Dry in the shade in small bunches or on a screen, never in direct sun.
herbHarvest.notes: Harvest on a dry day around noon, before full bloom. Dry in the shade in bunches or on a screen — never in direct sun.

tomatoSuckering.title: Time to sucker your tomato
tomatoSuckering.subtitle: Pinch out the side shoots weekly — energy to fruit; in August pinch the tip to ripen
tomatoSuckering.primaryInstr: Weekly snap off the side shoots (suckers) in the leaf axils — only for indeterminate (tall) varieties. Bush/determinate tomatoes are not suckered.
tomatoSuckering.apicalInstr: Pinch out the top of the main stem — the plant stops growing upward and channels energy into ripening the remaining fruit before season's end.
tomatoSuckering.primaryNotes: Snap off the side shoots weekly — energy to fruit, not leaves. Only for indeterminate types.
tomatoSuckering.apicalNotes: Pinch the tip of the stem — helps the remaining fruit ripen before season's end.

fruitNetting.title: Time to cover the crop
fruitNetting.subtitle: Pull netting over bushes and trees so birds don't get the harvest
fruitNetting.instr: Pull the netting JUST BEFORE the fruit colours — too late = birds get there first. Anchor the net to the ground so birds can't slip underneath.
fruitNetting.notes: Bird netting — pull the net just before the fruit colours and anchor it to the ground.

pinching.title: Time to pinch the tips
pinching.subtitle: Pinching + Chelsea chop — for a denser, bushier plant with 2–4× more blooms
pinching.instrChelseaChop: Cut the whole plant back by about 1/3 — it pushes out more side shoots and shifts flowering 2–3 weeks later.
pinching.instrAnnualPinch: Pinch out the top 2–3 leaves above a node before the flower stalk forms — it pushes out side shoots for a bushy clump.

hardeningOff.title: Time to harden off the seedlings
hardeningOff.subtitle: 7–14 day gradual exposure outdoors — without hardening, sun burns leaves and thermal shock stalls growth 2–3 weeks
hardeningOff.instrCoolSeason: Start in April — days 1–2 in shade 1–2 h, gradually add sun. Plant out in early May.
hardeningOff.instrWarmSeason: Start after the ice saints (~May 15) — 7–14 days gradually, days 1–2 shade 1–2 h, days 11–14 overnight outside (if T_min > 10 °C).
hardeningOff.instrHeatLoving: Start in June, once T_min > 10 °C overnight — squash and zucchini need stable warmth, otherwise growth stalls.

lateSowing.title: Time for a second sowing
lateSowing.subtitle: After the main harvest, reuse empty beds for a late/winter crop — otherwise weeds take over
lateSowing.instrMidsummer: Sow in July — still time to mature for harvest in August–September. In colder zones aim for mid-month, not late.
lateSowing.instrLateSummer: Sow in August — cool-season crops germinate in the cool, harvest 9–11 or overwinter. Water the bed before sowing.
lateSowing.instrEarlyAutumn: Sow in September — winter lettuce tolerates light frost, harvest gradually through November. Keep the bed moist until emergence.
lateSowing.instrAutumn: Plant overwintering garlic in October — cloves 5 cm deep, pointed end up. They root before frost, harvest in June–July next year.

seedSaving.title: Time to collect seeds
seedSaving.subtitle: Autumn self-seeding — gather seeds and capsules for next season
seedSaving.instrEarlyAutumn: Seeds must be DRY on the plant but not yet shattered. Clip umbels / heads into a paper bag on a dry day.
seedSaving.instrMidAutumn: Let the seed heads ripen on the plant to brown. Harvest on a dry day so the seeds aren't damp.
seedSaving.instrLateAutumn: Capsules must be DRY but still closed (otherwise the seeds drop). Dry further in a cool airy spot, store in paper.

strawberryStrawing.title: Time to straw-mulch the strawberries
strawberryStrawing.subtitle: Straw keeps fruit off the ground — clean, no rot, no slugs
strawberryStrawing.action: Spread dry straw between the plants
strawberryStrawing.instr: Spread dry straw between plants and under the trusses JUST BEFORE the fruit starts to ripen — keeps berries off the soil (no splash, no rot), repels slugs, reduces evaporation and keeps root-zone moisture steady.
strawberryStrawing.notes: Spread dry straw between plants under the trusses — keeps fruit off the ground, deters slugs, holds moisture.

strawberryRenewal.title: Time to renew strawberries
strawberryRenewal.subtitle: After the harvest, cut back old leaves and remove excess runners
strawberryRenewal.action: Cut back leaves on {{plant}} and remove runners
strawberryRenewal.instr: Cut leaves LOW ABOVE THE CROWN (don't damage the centre of the clump) and remove excess runners (stolons) — the plant pushes out fresh healthy leaves and sets flower buds for next season.
strawberryRenewal.notes: Cut leaves low above the crown + remove excess runners (stolons).

springMulching.title: Time to mulch
springMulching.subtitle: 5–8 cm of bark / wood chips — 30–40% more soil moisture, less weeds, root insulation
springMulching.instrPerennial: Apply 5–8 cm of shredded bark / wood chips / compost around the clump — NOT up against the stem (the crown needs air). Mulch only moist soil; water dry soil first.
springMulching.instrWoody: Apply 5–8 cm of shredded bark / wood chips / compost around the root zone — NOT up against the trunk (it would cause rot). Mulch only moist soil; water dry soil first.

winterPrep.title: Get ready for winter
winterPrep.notesLift: Lift the tubers/corms before the first frost and store above 5 °C.
winterPrep.notesProtect: Before the first frost, cover, wrap or move to a frost-free shelter.

idealDay.tooltipDry: This day is dry and calm — ideal for pruning and spraying (rain washes off sprays, damp cuts invite fungal infection).
idealDay.tooltipMild: A mild, frost-free day — ideal for transplanting and planting.
idealDay.tooltipPostrain: Rain is forecast for this day — postpone watering or fertilizing to a drier day (rain will water, but would wash granules away).

companionWarn.badBody: Growing nearby: {{neighbors}}. This combination doesn't get along — consider replanting.
companionWarn.goodSuffix: On the bright side, it does well next to: {{neighbors}}.
companionWarn.goodBody: Growing nearby: {{neighbors}} — these plants help each other.

cropRotation.warnRepeat: {{family}} repeat in this bed — rotate to another family.
cropRotation.plantWarnBody: The same family grew here recently — {{family}} ({{year}}). Repeating it exhausts the soil and builds up disease; consider rotating.

greenManure.title: Sow a cover crop
greenManure.summary_one: {{count}} freed bed is ready for a cover crop.
greenManure.summary_other: {{count}} freed beds are ready for a cover crop.
greenManure.taskTitle: Sow a cover crop in bed {{bed}}
greenManure.notesFrostKill: Sow mustard/phacelia into the freed bed — it winter-kills on its own, just dig or mulch it in next spring.
greenManure.notesOverwinter: Sow winter rye/clover into the freed bed — it overwinters; dig it in ~3 weeks before spring planting.

soilPh.limeSummary_one: {{count}} brassica bed is due for autumn liming.
soilPh.limeSummary_other: {{count}} brassica beds are due for autumn liming.
soilPh.limeNotes: Lime the bed in autumn before brassicas — raises pH and prevents clubroot (Plasmodiophora). Apply lime separately from nitrogen feeding.
soilPh.acidSubtitle: Acid-loving plant — keep the soil acidic
soilPh.acidInstr: Work in elemental sulfur or peat in autumn — sulfur converts over winter (target pH ~4.5–5.5).
soilPh.acidNotes: Acidify the site (elemental sulfur / peat) — blueberry, rhododendron, heather and the like want acidic soil (pH ~4.5–5.5). Keep separate from liming.

woodRipening.title: Harden the wood for winter
woodRipening.subtitle: Stop nitrogen — switch to potassium-phosphorus (PK) feed to ripen the wood
woodRipening.instr: Stop nitrogen — potassium helps this year's wood ripen and harden against frost. Use potassium sulfate, wood ash or another PK fertilizer.
woodRipening.notes: Stop nitrogen — PK fertilizer for wood ripening and frost hardiness.

polygonOnboarding.title: ✂️ Outline your garden
polygonOnboarding.add: Add a point (tap a dot between corners)
polygonOnboarding.tipDot: Tip: tap the white dot midway on a side to add a point.
